kill_fact: No kill_fact found. The adversarial attack vectors all FAIL to break the (bear/PASS) thesis: (a) STALE-ANCHOR — NO; sop_fair_value is built on CURRENT data: Q1-2026 actuals (filed in the 2026-05-05 8-K), reaffirmed FY2026 guidance, the post-restructuring Amazon FTM contract (Jun-2025), and the announced Cornerstone deal — not pre-event/pre-guidance financials. (b) NUMBER-TRUTH — every load-bearing figure verifies against primary SEC 8-Ks and company IR: Q1-26 adj EBITDA $473M, adj FCF $350M, GAAP NI +$63M; FY2026 guide $1,750-2,050M EBITDA / $980-1,180M FCF (ex-Cornerstone); net leverage 3.1x ex-Cornerstone with a 3.5x YE2026 target; Cornerstone 2,451 MW ($3.45B price, $4B raised); Amazon 1,920 MW FTM through 2042 (~$18B lifetime rev, ramp 840-1,200 MW by 2029 / 1,680-1,920 MW by 2032); ~45.4M shares; price ~$435. (c) THESIS-WEAKNESS — CONFIRMED in the bear's favor: the '1+ GW pipeline' is INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED as still UNSIGNED (no new hyperscaler PPA beyond Amazon found in any primary/news source as of Jun-2026), so the marginal equity dollar does rest on an unearned option; single-customer (Amazon) + single-asset (Susquehanna) + single-macro-factor (AI-capex) concentration all hold. (d) HIDDEN-DISQUALIFIER — the Cornerstone $4B debt raised AHEAD of regulatory approvals (FERC/IURC pending; HSR cleared Mar-2026) pinning pro-forma leverage at the ~3.5x ceiling is real and verified. The bear case (C / PASS / watchlist-for-a-flush) SURVIVES the kill-check intact.
corrections: One non-load-bearing correction: the bull_thesis/dossier state the new notes refinanced to '~6.25%'. The actual Cornerstone financing (priced 2026-04-17) is $1.5B of 6.125% senior notes due 2031 + $2.5B of 6.375% senior notes due 2033 (blended ~6.3%), redeeming $1.2B of 8.625% notes. Cosmetic; does not change leverage or the verdict. Also note the Cornerstone purchase price is $3.45B (the $4B figure is gross debt RAISED, which also funds the 8.625% redemption) — the dossier conflates the two loosely but the bear's leverage-to-ceiling point is correct.
evidence: SEC 8-K, Talen Energy Q1 2026 earnings release (filed 2026-05-05): adj EBITDA $473M, adj FCF $350M, GAAP NI $63M; FY2026 reaffirmed $1,750-2,050M EBITDA / $980-1,180M FCF excl. Cornerstone; net leverage 3.1x (sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001622536/000162253626000035)

SEC 8-K, Talen senior notes pricing (2026-04-17): $1.5B 6.125% notes due 2031 + $2.5B 6.375% notes due 2033 (~$4B) to fund the $3.45B Cornerstone 2,451 MW acquisition (Lawrenceburg 1,120 / Waterford 875 / Darby 456) and redeem $1.2B 8.625% notes; close early H2-2026, FERC/IURC pending, HSR cleared Mar-2026 (sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1622536/000162253626000030)

SEC 8-K / Talen IR (2025-06-11): Amazon AWS 1,920 MW front-of-the-meter PPA from Susquehanna through 2042, ~$18B lifetime revenue, ramp 840-1,200 MW by 2029 and 1,680-1,920 MW by 2032; restructured from behind-the-meter after the Nov-2024 FERC rejection (ir.talenenergy.com / sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001622536/000162828025030559)

Web/news verification (Jun-2026): no NEW signed hyperscaler/data-center PPA beyond the Amazon deal located in any primary or news source — the 'several 1+ GW' pipeline remains UNSIGNED; ~45.4M shares outstanding, price ~$435 — corroborating the bear's 'option already priced, not yet earned' core claim (powermag.com; nasdaq.com TLN)
